description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer featuring a robust, cylindrical black metal head with flat striking faces on both ends. The head is solid and slightly textured, showing subtle surface irregularities that suggest durability and frequent use. The handle is made of light brown wood, straight and smooth, with a rounded end for a secure grip. The junction between the handle and the head is tightly fitted, with the wooden handle inserted through the center of the metal head. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and strength, suitable for breaking concrete, driving stakes, or demolition tasks in urban environments. No visible branding, markings, or additional accessories are present on the hammer.
description: A sturdy sledgehammer with a black metal head and a straight wooden handle, designed for heavy-duty striking tasks. The tool is utilitarian, with a smooth handle and a solid, slightly textured head.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the black metal head and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the cylindrical profile of the head and the narrow width of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the opposite flat striking face of the head and the handle visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the cylindrical head profile and the handle's side.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle, smooth, cylindrical, with a rounded end and no visible grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: sledgehammer type, cylindrical black steel head with two flat striking faces and no claws or peens
